title: Data Analyst
job_description:
About the job

Job Description

Deliver insights and analysis using statistical methods, data visualization, and business use cases to support decision making
Design, develop, and maintain dashboards and reports to monitor business, product, and customer performance
Work closely with cross-functional teams to understand business needs and translate them into analytical solutions
Identify data requirements, validate data quality, and ensure accuracy and consistency of analysis outputs
Transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that can be understood by non-technical stakeholders
Create and maintain documentation covering data sources, assumptions, methodologies, and analysis processes
Continuously improve data analysis workflows, reporting standards, and best practices




Requirements

Minimum Bachelor’s degree S1 in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or a related field or equivalent practical experience
Minimum 2 years of experience in analyzing product, customer, or business data
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
Proficient in SQL and relational databases such as PostgreSQL or similar
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools such as Tableau or Power BI
Experience using programming languages for data analysis such as Python
Ability to translate business requirements into clear, non-technical insights and produce comprehensive analytical reports
Strong attention to detail and ability to work effectively with cross-functional stakeholders

title: Data Scientist
job_description:
About the job

PT Artajasa Pembayaran Elektronis is a leading provider of electronic payment solutions in Indonesia. One of our brand, “ATM Bersama” is the pioneer of the first and largest electronic payment system service in Indonesia, which has been connected with more than 90 member financial institutions, both Banks and Non-Banks (Fintech, etc) in Indonesia. As we continue to innovate, we are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team and transform data into actionable insights that drive strategic decisions and business growth.




About the Role

We are looking for a Data Scientist to analyze large-scale transaction data, develop predictive models, and generate actionable insights to support business decisions. This role is critical in transforming data into strategic value, particularly in areas such as customer behavior, risk detection, and operational efficiency.




Key Responsibilities

Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Develop and implement predictive models and machine learning algorithms.
Perform data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model evaluation.
Collaborate with business, product, and technology teams to translate data into actionable strategies.
Build data visualizations and dashboards to communicate insights effectively.
Support use cases such as fraud detection, customer segmentation, and performance optimization.
Ensure data quality, integrity, and proper documentation of models and processes.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or related field.
Minimum 2–4 years of experience in data science, analytics, or machine learning roles.
Strong proficiency in programming languages such as Python or R.
Experience with data analysis tools and libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn).
Familiarity with SQL and working with large datasets.
Knowledge of machine learning techniques and statistical modeling.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Experience in financial services or payment industry is a plus.

Equal Opportunity Employer

title: Investment Banking Intern
job_description:
About the job

Investment Banking Internship




With Exposure to Investment Banking, Private Equity, Hedge Funds & Global Capital Markets




Are you a driven college student or recent graduate who wants to break into investment banking, private equity, or hedge funds? Do you want real exposure to deal analysis, valuation, pitchbook prep, market research, and capital strategy—while also learning core skills used by elite bankers and investment professionals?




This internship is designed as a career launchpad for students aiming for top roles in finance. As part of our global investment platform spanning New York, Connecticut, London, Zurich, and Stockholm, you will gain experience that goes far beyond the typical classroom or club environment.




You will also receive exposure to select training materials from Swiss Finance Academy, an established, elite and selective international finance education brand known for its intensive, real-world preparation for careers in investment banking and institutional asset management, with its alumni base at all major investment banks worldwide.




This internship is highly selective and unpaid, structured similarly to practical training programs across the U.S. and Europe.




About the Company




We are a global investment management group with deep experience in investment banking, capital markets, global macro, and quantitative strategies. We work across multiple financial hubs including New York, London, and Zurich. Our educational affiliate, Swiss Finance Academy, has developed elite training content used by students worldwide. Our Swiss entity Swiss Finance Education GmbH operates exclusively at SwissFinanceInstitute.AI site. Notice the .AI in our official and authentic site!




Successful interns may be considered for additional internships in affiliate hedge funds, trading, investment banking - PPD, investor relations, hedge fund research, and capital outreach roles. There are international placement opportunities in limited cases.




About the Internship

You will work on real, finance-focused tasks and gain an understanding of how deals, firms, and markets are analyzed at a professional level. You will learn how investment bankers and institutional investors evaluate companies, structure transactions, and communicate ideas.

This is ideal for students seeking to secure future roles in investment banking, private equity, private credit, hedge funds, or corporate finance.




Responsibilities

Build and update financial models (DCF, comparables, precedent transactions, LBO basics)
Assist in preparing pitchbooks, teasers, and investor presentation materials
Conduct industry and company research across multiple sectors
Analyze financial statements and earnings reports
Support valuation exercises and deal case studies
Review and repurpose Swiss Finance Academy educational content into polished research memos
Contribute to investment memos and internal briefs used by senior team members
Assist with investor outreach research and capital allocator mapping if strong fit




We Are Looking for Candidates Who

Are pursuing a degree in finance, economics, business, engineering, or related fields
Have strong interest in investment banking, private equity, or asset management
Are analytical, detail-oriented, and able to work independently
Want to develop real-world deal analysis and financial modeling experience
Are ambitious, driven, and eager to learn in a high-performance environment
Are comfortable communicating professionally in a remote setting




What You Will Gain

Real exposure to investment banking deal workflows
Hands-on practice in financial modeling, valuation, and market research
Experience preparing professional-quality pitch materials and analysis decks
Your development through elite and selective Swiss Finance Academy training material
Resume-ready deliverables that differentiate you from typical applicants
Insight into hedge fund allocator psychology and capital markets dynamics
Potential for follow-on internships in investor relations, research, or other roles upon strong performance




Internship Details

Location: Remote
Schedule: Flexible; part-time or full-time depending on availability
Compensation: Unpaid, due to significant practical training and skill development provided
Eligibility: Undergraduate or graduate students including MBAs, recent grads and career changers as well are welcome to apply
Start Dates: Rolling







Follow us on Instagram and LinkedIn for industry insights, career guidance, and exclusive opportunities in investment banking and hedge funds.







Applicant Verification & Trademark Notice




How do I verify I'm applying to the correct Swiss Finance Institute?

Swiss Finance Institute operates exclusively at swissfinanceinstitute.ai and holds registered trademark protection in the United States and internationally through our Swiss entity, Swiss Finance Education GMBH.

Our trademark registrations are publicly verifiable and include:

USPTO Serial Numbers: 85671404, 78806277, 85688723 (and additional registrations)
Status: Incontestable on the Principal Register of the United States Patent and Trademark Office
Classes: Financial education and related services
Priority: Established rights dating back nearly 25 years.




Verify our registrations:

United States Patent and Trademark Office public database: uspto.gov

Search: "Swiss Finance Institute" or use serial numbers above

US Government Trademark Website: Swiss Finance Institute

Additional Brand Registrations




Important Notice to Applicants

Swiss Finance Institute operates exclusively at swissfinanceinstitute.ai in the US and most other jurisdictions. This is our only authorized domain and official platform by Swiss Finance Education GmbH.

We are the registered owner of "Swiss Finance Institute," "Swiss Finance," and related marks with incontestable trademark status in the United States. We maintain exclusive rights to these marks and actively monitor and enforce our intellectual property.




Before applying, please verify:

You are viewing this posting on our verified LinkedIn company page
Any communications reference our official domain: swissfinanceinstitute.AI
Our trademark ownership is confirmed via public USPTO records

We do not authorize or affiliate with any other entity, domain, or platform using "Swiss Finance Institute," "Swiss Finance," or confusingly similar marks.

title: IGNITE Internship: Operations (June Intake)
job_description:
About the job

FedEx IGNITE Operations Internship (June 2026 intake)

FedEx IGNITE Internship: Fuel Your Future!

Location: Cengkareng, Indonesia

Intake: June 2026

Duration: 12 Weeks




About the IGNITE Internship Program

IGNITE is a standard12-week internship at FedEx designed to give high-potential youth the opportunity to gain hands-on exposure to the world of work.

Through real business projects, mentorship from experienced professionals, and curated learning experiences, this structured program is designed to help you learn, grow, and make a real impact from day one.




What You’ll Gain

Real-World Experience – Work on meaningful projects that drive business impact and learn best practices by shadowing experienced professionals.
Mentorship & Learning – Receive mentorship from industry professionals to accelerate your growth.
Collaboration & Innovation – Engage with cross-functional teams and like-minded peers.
Professional Development – Build industry-relevant skills and enhance your employability through programs and workshops.
Networking Opportunities – Expand your professional network by engaging with peers and professionals across the region.




Explore our available Operations internship roles below. Candidates with a Bachelor's Degree from Computer Science, Engineering, Data Analytics, Information Systems, are encouraged to apply.







Global Services Provider

Develop a Power BI dashboard to track and visualize clearance performance across Asia Pacific
Analyse clearance data to improve metrics and support data-driven decision-making
Develop tools and solutions to automate administrative steps within the contra settlement process
Identify process gaps and propose automation opportunities to improve efficiency and accuracy
Support continuous improvement initiatives through clear reporting, insights, and documentation







Operations Excellence

Support Indonesia Operations Excellence (OE) in conducting weekly deep dives into clearance service performance
Contribute to initiatives that drive clearance excellence and operational efficiency
Design and develop a Payment Before Invoicing (PBI) dashboard to enhance visibility and management of Cost Per Package (Cost Per Package) performance at both Indonesia and Singapore levels
Provide data-driven insights and reporting to support performance tracking and continuous improvement







Pick Up and Delivery

Contribute to automation and continuous improvement initiatives within Pick-Up & Delivery operations
Conduct data analysis on shipment data, weekly performance, and operational productivity
Support the creation of weekly and monthly performance dashboards to enhance decision-making
Collaborate on the development of Power BI dashboard to strengthen visibility of operational performance
Assist in process workflow documentation and support testing and rollout of automation or digital tools







Who We’re Looking For

Available to join us in June 2026
Curious and eager to learn
Proactive and adaptable in a fast-paced environment
Team-oriented with strong communication skills
Demonstrate critical thinking and problem-solving abilities
Able to communicate effectively in English
Able to commit for at least 12 weeks

Note: Open to International Students with valid student pass & VISA.




Application Process

Submit your application online
Interview – Shortlisted candidates will be going through an interview.
Offer & Onboarding – Successful candidates will join the program in June.




Who we are

At FedEx, moving the world doesn’t only mean delivering for our customers around the globe. Moving the world is also about creating what’s next. We are Team FedEx and we are 500,000+ people strong around the world. The work we all do comes down to one purpose: to provide our customers with outstanding experiences as we connect the world to what’s next.




This isn’t a place to get just a job. Here, you get a career for life. It’s a place to enjoy constant change, growth, and progress in your career. Our willingness to innovate and transform is what keeps us at the forefront of our industry. With curiosity, inventiveness, and entrepreneurial spirit, we keep finding new and better ways to deliver results – for our customers, for our people and for the planet.




Here, you will have the flexibility and empowerment to reimagine the new, spread your wings and see your impact. By doing so, we are motivated to be better together. And together, we will continue to drive and deliver excellence.




Our Values

We take pride in being a people-first company, where our employees feel safe, valued and respected. Our culture values empower us to deliver great results.




With one FedEx culture, we:

Take care of each other | Commit to do good | Own outstanding | Drive business results | Create what’s next




Awards

FedEx has consistently ranked among the top 20 in the “World’s Most Admired Companies” report, published in FORTUNE magazine, since 2001
Ethisphere named FedEx as one of World’s Most Ethical Companies® in 2023
HR Asia Best Companies to Work For in Asia

title: Intern
job_description:
About the job

Job Description

Job Overview:

We are seeking a motivated Intern to join our team and assist in dimension control for various fabrication and construction projects. This internship provides an excellent opportunity for active student to gain hands-on experience dimension control in a dynamic environment.

Responsibilities

Key Tasks and Responsibilities:


Under supervision and with guidance, you will be involved in learning and assisting with:
Pipe Mill Dimension Control:
Assisting in the inspection and checking of Can and Tubular components.
Braces Rack Dimension Control:
Helping check and ensure all braces meet dimension control standards.
Pile Rack Dimension Control:
Supporting inspections of Legs, Piles, caissons, buoyancy tanks, and all other elements related to jackets and decks.
Fabrication Shop / East Shop:
Learning how to perform dimension checks in the Fabrication and East Shops, specifically related to jackets and decks.
Pipe Shop Dimension Control:
Assisting in inspecting fabricated pipe spools for dimensional accuracy.
Deck and Erection Area Support:
Helping with the dimension control of deck assemblies, layout footprint, geometry calculations, and reference line surveys. Observing load-out operations and settlement surveys.
Assisting with jacket assemblies, footprint calculations, and layout activities for jackets. 
Observing load-out operations and assisting with surveys during project stages.
Marine and Yard Facility Support:
Assisting Marine Base and Yard Facility staff with dimension control tasks as directed.
Learning and Adhering to Safety Standards:
Ensuring a high standard of safe working practices and maintaining productivity while learning.
Surveying Techniques:
Learning how to use various survey measurement equipment (e.g., Auto Level, Total Station) and understanding the basics of surveying methods.
Learning Codes and Specifications:
Gaining knowledge about implementing relevant Codes and Specifications in surveying and dimension control.
Collaborative Work:
Working with various departments and disciplines to gather and report necessary data during fabrication stages.
AutoCAD & Calculation Program:
Observing and learning how to operate AutoCAD and use calculation software to assist with dimension control tasks.

Qualifications

Essential Qualifications and Education:


Active college student in D3/D4/S1. (Geodesy, Civil Engineering)
Internship placements are available at the McDermott Batam Fabrication Yard. The internship is scheduled to start in June 2026, with 4 (four) months duration.
Applicants should not be participating in another internship program.
Applicants should have strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment.
Attention to detail and a willingness to follow instructions


About Us

Our ingenuity fuels daily life. Together, we’ve forged some of the most trusted partnerships across the energy value chain to make what was once just an idea a reality: laying subsea infrastructure thousands of feet below sea level, installing platforms hundreds of miles from shore, using our expertise to design and build offshore wind infrastructure, and reshaping the onshore landscape to deliver the energy products the world needs safely and sustainably.

For more than 100 years, we've been making the impossible possible. Today, we're driving the energy transition with more than 30,000 of the brightest minds across 54 countries.

title: GoTo Financial - Lending Risk Analyst Acceleration Program
job_description:
About the job

About The Role

GoTo Financial is seeking ambitious talents to join our Lending Risk Analyst Acceleration Program. This is not just a job, it is a structured rotational program designed to build the next generation of risk leaders in the digital finance space—offering a faster track to career progression for high-potential individuals.

As a trainee, you will embark on a hands-on on-the-job learning journey, rotating through specialized functions within our Lending Risk division. You will gain deep expertise in identifying, assessing, and mitigating the most critical risks in our financial ecosystems. By working alongside industry experts, you will learn how to balance innovation with prudence while accelerating your professional growth in one of Southeast Asia's fastest-growing fintech environments.

Your work will help reshaping the future of thelending industry. If you’re excited about the challenge, this role is for you!”

What You Will Do


Risk Analytics & Insights: Leverage large datasets to extract actionable insights, identify emerging risk trends, monitor portfolio performance, and support the development of data-driven risk strategies across credit and fraud domains.
Rotational Exposure: Participate in structured rotations across different functions within Credit Risk and Fraud Risk to understand the end-to-end risk management lifecycle.
Risk Monitoring: Support the team in daily risk monitoring activities, including tracking key risk indicators (KRIs) and generating regular risk reports for stakeholders.
Policy & Process Improvement: Collaborate with team leads to review and enhance risk policies, standard operating procedures, and internal controls to ensure they remain robust and scalable.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with STLs, Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure risk considerations are integrated into new product launches and features.
Project Support: Contribute to special projects and initiatives aimed at modernizing the risk management framework, including the exploration of new tools or methodologies.


What You Will Need:


Educational Background: Fresh graduate or recent graduate (graduated within the last 2 years) with a Bachelor's or Master's degree from a reputable university.
Field of Study: Strong academic track record in Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, Finance, or other related quantitative fields.
Data Proficiency: Analytical skills with hands-on experience in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, SQL, Python, or R) is highly preferred.
Problem-Solving Mindset: Logical thinking and structured problem-solving skills with a passion for data analysis and tackling complex challenges.
Adaptability: Eagerness to learn quickly and adapt to new challenges in a fast-paced, ever-changing fintech landscape.
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English; ability to articulate complex ideas clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
Attitude: Highly motivated, detail-oriented, and possessing a strong sense of integrity and ownership.


About The Team

You will be part of the GoPay Lending Risk Team, responsible for supporting the risk management of our lending products. The team plays a key role in ensuring that our lending business grows sustainably while maintaining strong risk control.

The team focuses on identifying, assessing, and managing credit and operational risks across the lending lifecycle, including risk strategy, policy development, monitoring portfolio performance, and implementing risk mitigation measures.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams, including Product, Data, Engineering, and Business teams, and collaborate with stakeholders across different regions. This provides an opportunity to contribute to impactful initiatives while gaining exposure to a dynamic and fast-growing fintech environment.

About GoTo Group

GoTo is the largest digital ecosystem in Indonesia. GoTo's mission is to 'empower progress' by offering technology infrastructure and solutions that help everyone to access and thrive in the digital economy.

The GoTo ecosystem provides a wide range of services, including mobility, delivery, payments, financial services, and technology solutions for merchants. The ecosystem also provides e-commerce services through Tokopedia and banking services through its partnership with Bank Jago.

About Gojek

Gojek is Southeast Asia’s leading on-demand platform and pioneer of the multi-service ecosystem with over 2.5 million driver partners across the regions offering a wide range of services such as transportation, food delivery, logistics and more. With its mission to create impact at scale, Gojek is committed to resolving consumer problems and raising standards of living by connecting consumers to the best providers of goods and services in the market.

About GoTo Financial

GoTo Financial accelerates financial inclusion through its leading financial services and merchants solutions. Its consumer services include GoPay and GoPayLater and serve businesses of all sizes through Midtrans, Moka, GoBiz Plus, GoBiz, and Selly. With its trusted and inclusive ecosystem of products, GoTo Financial is open to new growth opportunities and aims to empower everyone to Make It Happen, Make It Together, Make It Last.

GoTo and its business units, including Gojek and GoToFinancial ("GoTo") only post job opportunities on our official channels on our respective company websites and on LinkedIn. GoTo is not liable for any job postings or job offers that did not originate from us. You should conduct your own due diligence to prevent being victims of any fake job scams, if they did not originate from GoTo's official recruitment channels.

title: Data Analyst
job_description:
About the job

At Catalyst, People are the heartbeat of our company. We believe that good quality people will have a positive impact on our business. We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our growing team. If you are passionate about being part of the team, building some of the most critical products, and Working alongside teams in the industry across product, engineering, and design, Let’s join us!







Job Description 

Build data dashboards to monitor company performance and efficiency metrics
Working with data engineering & data analytics teams to prioritize to provide, integrate and govern all company data
Support the business transformation from manual data entry systems to an automated, end-to-end structured data system
Design, build, and drive adoption of data dashboards for the tech-enabled Operations team, such as pipeline management and conversion funnel metrics
Build weekly/monthly analytical reports, leveraging data from our internal product
Synthesize business intelligence to provide critical business insights for the Operational Excellence function
Partner with other business divisions (operations, growth, etc) to generate ad-hoc data analysis requests and diagnose root causes of operational pain points (e.g. root cause of the decline in sales team activity or reduction in time to fill)

Our team is focused on understanding user experiences and partnering with Business, Product, Design, and Tech teams to achieve the right outcomes. We are integral to product development, from understanding user journeys with UX designers, to developing hypotheses, through to post-rollout optimisation.

Get to Know the Role

You will use data to understand user needs and be the de-facto Voice of the Customer (users, driver-partners, merchants, agents) across Lending's suite of products. You will leverage data to unveil insights to help launch, improve and scale our products. You will champion data-driven decision-making within GFin and partner with Product Managers, Business Owners, UX Designers, and Engineers to design analytical projects that drive the Product roadmap. You will provide thought leadership and generate data-driven hypotheses to solve key Product and Business problems, and drive our GFin strategies forward. You will design, measure, and recommend A/B tests and multivariate experiments, including layout optimisation, UI/UX efficacy, algorithm effectiveness, and API performance.
You will analyse clickstream and transactional data to uncover insights on user behaviour and drive product metrics. You will manage instrumentation for all feature releases within assigned tech families within GFin.
You will develop self-serve solutions for stakeholders that are scalable and automated to handle a product environment. You will drive plans involving Large Language Models or Generative Artificial Intelligence.


Qualifications

You have at least 4 years of experience in Analytics, Business Intelligence, or Data Science, in a Tech (FinTech or E-Commerce) company.

You have experience designing, running, and analysing A/B tests and product experiments, with knowledge of hypothesis testing and the basic principles of Design of Experiments (DoE).

We consider all candidates fairly and equally regardless of nationality, ethnicity, race, religion, age, gender, family commitments, physical and mental impairments or disabilities, and other attributes that make them unique.

title: AnyLab Internship Program: AI Agents and Workflow Automation
job_description:
About the job

ANYLAB INTERNSHIP PROGRAM

▎ What Is AnyLab?

An AI Implementation Lab based in AnyMind Indonesia office. A team that builds AI agent systems to automate real business operations and deploys them into production environments. Join the founding squad of an AI implementation team and build autonomous AI agent systems that solve real business problems — working daily alongside the technical architect.

▎What Interns Will Do - Core Mission

Build AI agent systems that automate business operations at scale: Design, implement, and deploy multi-agent architectures where multiple specialized AI agents collaborate through structured data protocols to autonomously complete complex tasks.

Interns will work on a range of business automation projects, matched to business priorities and their own interests and strengths. These projects can include automating social media analysis, drafting business proposals and strategies, automating consumer research and insight generation, creating AI-powered marketing creatives with brand checks, building systems that aggregate and report data from multiple sources, and exploring new automation-based solutions or products

▎What Interns Will Learn

🏗 Multi-Agent System Design: Decompose complex workflows into specialized AI agents with clear responsibilities and communication protocols.

🛡 Harness Engineering: Build validation, error recovery, state management, and quality enforcement systems around AI agents — the discipline that separates production AI from demos.

🤖 AI-Directed Development: Use AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) as your primary development tool. Think in architecture, not syntax.

🎯 Context Engineering: Design what information to feed AI agents and when — the art of minimal, precise context injection.

📊 Evaluation & Quality Systems: Build quantitative scoring rubrics, golden datasets, and automated feedback loops to measure and enforce output quality.

⚡ Hybrid Architecture Design: Know when to use AI vs. regex vs. solvers vs. rules-based logic — choose the right tool for each part of a pipeline.

▎ What Are We Looking For


Currently studying in or recently graduated from a reputable university, preferably in Business, Marketing, Data/Computer Science, or a related field.
Strong interest in business automation and AI agents for marketing, research, and reporting use cases.
Ability to analyze data and turn findings into clear, actionable insights or simple reports.
Basic technical skills for working with AI/automation tools (e.g., Python, SQL, or low-code/agentic builders like n8n/Make is a plus, not a must).
Strong problem-solving skills and willingness to experiment with new tools and workflows.
Clear communication skills to document processes, explain findings, and collaborate with business stakeholders.

We’re looking for a Finance Intern to support our Budget & Treasury function. You’ll work closely with the finance team to ensure our financial activities run smoothly and efficiently while gaining exposure to real-world finance operations in a fast-growing company.




About Flip

Rafi, Luqman, and Anjar, who were college friends in Universitas Indonesia, started Flip as a project in 2015 to transfer payments to each other at a fraction of what banks would charge them. They are pioneers in the Indonesian market, with their technology now helping millions of Indonesians, both individuals and businesses, carry out bank-to-bank money transfers through a reliable and seamless app.




After nine years of operations, Flip has helped Indonesians transfer money worth several trillions of rupiah and has received double-digit funding from respectable investors such as Sequoia India, Insight Partner, and Insignia. Flip’s ultimate mission is to give Indonesians access to one of the most progressive and fairest financial services in the world.




At Flip, we always strive to provide the fairest place for you to work, learn, and grow with talented and fun people in various opportunities to advance your career and get fair rewards. job_description:
About the job

About The Job Data Analyst (Marine Engineering)

Meratus Group is a leading integrated maritime and logistics operator in Indonesia, pioneering innovative solutions that drive efficiency and sustainability in the industry. With a rich history dating back to 1957, Meratus has evolved into a powerhouse, operating over 45 shipping routes, a fleet of 100 vessels, and a network of container terminals and logistics centers.

At Meratus, we are committed to digitalization, innovation, and transformation, ensuring seamless logistics and maritime services across Indonesia and Southeast Asia. Our customer-centric approach and agile operations empower businesses to navigate complex supply chain challenges with confidence.

We take pride in fostering a dynamic and inclusive workplace, where talented professionals can thrive and contribute to shaping the future of maritime logistics. Join us and be part of a team that is redefining industry standards while making a meaningful impact on global trade and sustainability.

 Position Overview

 This position is responsible for collecting, monitoring, analyzing, and recording all relevant data related to vessel usage from the Ship Management division.

 Responsibilities


Monitor, analyze, and record consumption reports for Main Engines, Auxiliary Engines, and other machinery across various platforms; assist management in decision-making based on summarized data.
Monitor, analyze, and record engine performance reports for each vessel; provide recommendations regarding data quality to ensure continuous performance improvement and compliance with company standards.
Collect and analyze monthly lube oil consumption reports for each vessel; support management in decision-making and contribute to initiatives aimed at improving lube oil consumption to meet company standards.
Record all off-hire incidents from each vessel and prepare summarized reports for management review.
Record all incidents of collision and grounding, and compile summarized reports for submission to management.
Record Fresh Water Generator production data and prepare summarized reports for management.
Provide support for improvement projects conducted by the Research & Training team.


Requirements


Minimum Bachelor Degree in Marine Engineering or related Engineering major.
Minimum 1 year of experience as Data Analyst.
Possess solid experience and knowledge in the engineering matters.
Willing to carry out administrative tasks.
Proper analytical thinking and communication skill.
Proficient in English.
Willing to be placed at Head Office, Surabaya.

title: Data Scientist - PayLater
job_description:
About the job

Job Description 

Job ID: MJ000085

Our data science team is responsible for solving complex problems and optimizing various aspects of Traveloka’s business. You will get a wide range of exciting opportunities to bring impact by helping to make decisions at scale across our various business lines, and get to work with the brightest minds in the industry. Join us in revolutionizing the travel industry through data!

Job Description


Partner with Risk, Product, Anti-Fraud, and Business Teams to understand risk challenges and define data-driven strategies.
Develop and enhance risk models for Probability of Default (PD)/Loss Given Default (LGD)/Exposure at Default (EAD) and anti-fraud models using machine learning and statistical techniques.
Design, validate, and maintain risk-based labels (e.g., DPD buckets, NPL status) to ensure consistency and reliability in model training.
Optimize credit strategies using simulation techniques to assess the impact of new policies on loan book performance.
Work closely with Data Engineering teams to ensure high-quality, scalable data pipelines for real-time/scheduled model predictions and performance monitoring.
Oversee and manage multiple credit risk modeling projects, ensuring alignment with business objectives, timelines, and regulatory requirements.
Communicate insights effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including risk managers and senior executives.


Job Requirements


Bachelor’s/Master’s/Ph.D. in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Finance, or related).
3+ years of experience in data science, preferably with a focus on credit risk modeling, loan portfolio analytics, or financial risk assessment.
Strong knowledge of credit risk/anti-fraud in the consumer lending domain.
Expertise in Python and machine learning/statistics libraries (scikit-learn, LGBM, XGBoost, etc).
Proficiency in SQL and data processing libraries for analyzing large-scale credit and transaction datasets.
Experience with cloud-based technologies (GCP preferred) and Docker for scalable model deployment.
Ability to translate business objectives into data-driven risk strategies and communicate findings effectively.


Good To Have


Experience with Deep Learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow) for alternative credit risk modeling.
Familiarity with alternative data sources for credit scoring (e.g., transaction data, behavioral data).
Knowledge of real-time credit decisioning systems and implementation of adaptive risk strategies.
Experience with A/B testing for credit policy optimization.
Experience in ML Lifecycle tools

Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar tools.
Familiarity with statistical analysis tools and techniques is a plus.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, including project and stakeholder management.

You will work with C-suite executives and senior managers, problem-solve on key issues as well as provide actionable recommendations leveraging your technological know-how and business sense.

You will play an active role in all aspects of a client project, typically working in teams of 3-5 consultants alongside experts and colleagues of all tenures, including developers, designers, data engineers, agile coaches and others. This includes gathering and analyzing information, formulating and testing hypotheses, and developing and communicating recommendations for client presentations. During some projects you’ll also present results to client management and implement recommendations in collaboration with client team members.

You will receive exceptional training as well as frequent coaching and mentoring from colleagues on your team. This support includes a Partner from your local office or practice assigned to you to help guide your career as well as up to five weeks of formal training in your first two years as an Associate. You'll work as part of our Tech & AI by McKinsey team in Jakarta. Tech & AI by McKinsey helps enterprises think strategically about how to leverage technology to create fundamental business change - from formulating tech-driven growth strategies to enabling more efficient operations, through the use of technology.

In a world where digital is the new standard, executives are increasingly looking for sharp advice on technology. With a team of 2,100 experts and consultants across 60 countries, Tech & AI by McKinsey works on key technology topics including big data, agile, cloud, cybersecurity, analytics and digital transformation. We serve a range of companies, including start-ups, the public sector and global Fortune 100 firms.

Bachelor's degree, ideally either in a business (economics, finance, management) or a technical field (engineering, computer science, data science)
2+ years of work experience preferred, leveraging analytics/AI for product growth and management, or in technical roles (data scientists / data engineers / software engineers) 
Outstanding record of academic achievement, with functional knowledge or passion in Digital, Technology and IT, as well as business and strategy
Proven record of leadership in a work setting and/or through extracurricular activities
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and effectively with people at all levels in an organization
Willingness to travel
Skills to communicate complex ideas effectively

title: Operations Associate, Middle Office, Jakarta
job_description:
About the job

Fitch Ratings’ Global Rating Services team is seeking Operations Associates to join the group in our Jakarta office.

As one of the world’s top three credit ratings agencies, Fitch Ratings plays a critical role in global capital markets by providing supplementary credit analysis, ratings, research, and commentary to financial market participants. For over 100 years, Fitch Ratings has been creating value for global markets through its rigorous analysis and deep expertise, which have resulted in a variety of market leading tools, methodologies, indices, research, and analytical products. Fitch Ratings is part of Fitch Group, a global leader in financial information services with operations in more than 30 countries, which also includes Fitch Solutions. With dual headquarters in London and New York, Fitch Group is owned by Hearst.

Becoming a member of our Fitch Ratings Asia Pacific team places you at the heart of a best-in-class organization, recognized with awards from InsuranceAsia, FinanceAsia and Corporate Treasurer for its excellence and leadership in the credit rating industry. Our accolades and awards in the region are a testament to our commitment to quality and innovation. As one of our colleagues, you'll engage with a diverse and dynamic community, gaining valuable experience and opportunities for growth in one of the world's largest financial markets.

About The Team

Fitch Ratings Operations is focused on developing an efficient operating environment for analytical teams by bringing consistency and standardisation to all operational aspects of the rating process.

This role requires collaboration with analytical teams, operational teams, IT squads, and other stakeholders to ensure that Middle Office workflow tasks, procedures, and applications effectively support business needs and comply with regulatory requirements.

How You’ll Make An Impact


Collaborate Effectively: Work seamlessly with Analytical teams, Operations teams, and IT squads to ensure adherence to correct workflow processes, maintain accurate data, and perform timely tasks that support ratings analysts using Fitch’s proprietary applications. 
Partner with Stakeholders: Team up with colleagues and other internal stakeholders to successfully complete ad hoc requests and special projects.
Identify Improvement Opportunities: Seek out and implement enhancements to current processes, procedures, and applications to provide the highest quality support to the global analytical community
Promote Compliance Awareness: Foster a comprehensive understanding of existing and emerging compliance and regulatory issues, along with related policies and procedures.


You May Be a Good Fit If


You have a bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) or 2+ years of experience in an operational role.
You demonstrate strong diligence, excellent attention to detail, and logical thinking.
You are highly motivated, flexible, proactive, and adaptable to change.
You possess excellent organisational and time management skills, along with strong problem-solving abilities.
You exhibit strong communication skills that enable effective collaboration across teams
You are a self-starter who can work well independently and thrive in a team environment.
You maintain a customer-focused mindset with a commitment to providing exceptional client service.


What Would Make You Stand Out


Experience working in a global financial services organisation.
Proficiency in data management and knowledge of fixed income.
A demonstrated interest and aptitude for leveraging information technology to enhance systems, with a growing focus on automation, machine learning, and artificial intelligence.
Fluent in Mandarin, both written and verbal.


Why Choose Fitch


Hybrid Work Environment: 3 days a week in office required based on your line of business and location
A Culture of Learning & Mobility: Dedicated trainings, leadership development and mentorship programs designed to ensure that your time at Fitch will be a continuous learning opportunity
Investing in Your Future: Tuition reimbursement programs that empower you to achieve your short and long-term goals
Promoting Health & Wellbeing: Comprehensive healthcare offerings that enable physical, mental, financial, social, and occupational wellbeing
Supportive Parenting Policies: Family-friendly policies, including a generous global parental leave plan, designed to help you balance career and family life effectively
Inclusive Work Environment: A collaborative workplace where all voices are valued, with Employee Resource Groups that unite and empower our colleagues around the globe
Dedication to Giving Back: Paid volunteer days, matched funding for donations and ample opportunities to volunteer in your community


Fitch is committed to providing global securities markets with objective, timely, independent and forward-looking credit opinions. To protect Fitch’s credibility and reputation, our employees must take every precaution to avoid conflicts of interest or any appearance of a conflict of interest. Should you be successful in the recruitment process at Fitch Ratings you will be asked to declare any securities holdings and other potential conflicts prior to commencing employment. If you, or your immediate family, have any holdings that may conflict with your work responsibilities, you may be asked to divest yourself of them before beginning work.

Fitch is proud to be an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. We evaluatequalified applicants without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, protected veteran status, and other statuses protected by law.

title: Search Operations Quality Analyst - Evaluation & Investigation
job_description:
About the job

Responsibilities
About Trust & Safety
Building a world where people can safely discover, create and connect. The Trust & Safety (T&S) team at TikTok helps ensure that our global online community is safe and empowered to create and enjoy content across all of our applications. We have invested heavily in human and machine-based moderation to remove harmful content quickly and often before it reaches our general community.

About the Team
The Search Operations team aims to improve search user experience, contribute significant DAU impact to the products and drive for increase in traffic and GMV.

The T&S evaluation and investigation team, working closely with the search team, taking the evaluation standards as the baseline and principles, and manually evaluating the search query and the corresponding search results page to reflect the real user experience of the search function.

The team is committed to providing search and evaluation services for international products, with resource support provided by different countries around the world, and global members conduct evaluations according to unified guidelines. The evaluation scope covers multiple search sections, such as basic search experience, recommended search, search advertising, and search security. As a bridge for business communication domestically and abroad, the team is responsible for key nodes such as standard writing, training Q&A, report output, and human efficiency management to effectively promote the execution and implementation of projects.

It is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content as part of the core role/as part of project/ in response to escalation requests/by chance.
This may occur in the form of images, video, and text related to every-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals.

Responsibilities:
- Quality inspection of labeled data on time and ensure the quality.
- Localize the labeling guidelines, support the guideline training, and align the guideline updates.
- Conduct calibration meetings with the labeling team in the appeal process: analyze different cases, record and evaluate their performance, and offer solutions and suggestions for the current problem to help the team improve the accuracy rate and alignment.
- Work closely with R&D (Research and Development) and PM (Product Manager) to provide feedback on data quality.
- Parts of this role will require supporting other products within the ByteDance suite. ByteDance is TikTok's parent company, whose mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications:
- Proficiency in English and Bahasa Indonesia. English will be utilized as the primary language for work, while Bahasa Indonesia will be used for market-specific communications.
- 1 year of experience as a Content Specialist or Content Reviewer.

Preferred Qualifications:
- Have completed a Bachelor’s degree or above.
- 1 year of experience as a Quality Assurance in Content or Customer Service.
- Good knowledge of search engines, and familiarity with social media platforms.
- An in-depth understanding of local culture and familiarity with the Internet usage habits of local users.

Trust & Safety
Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. 

TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. 

We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person-centred, innovative and integrated approach.


About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: Associate NDT Tech
job_description:
About the job

Job Description

Job Overview: 

The Associate NDT Tech / Radiation Associate Technician is responsible for managing all aspects of radiation safety within the organization. This role ensures compliance with regulations, promotes a strong safety culture, and implements effective radiation protection programs across operational activities.

Responsibilities

Key Tasks and Responsibilities:


 Implement safety measures by regularly monitoring radiation exposure levels to ensure they remain within regulatory limits. This includes maintaining and updating radiation dose records and transparently communicating, monitoring the results to personnel.
 Ensure that all McDermott and NDT subcontractor personnel are kept informed of current developments in radiation safety practices and technologies.
 Promptly report any incidents of exposure exceeding dose limits to the Permit Holder and initiate appropriate corrective actions.
 Monitor all activities involving radioactive materials, including routine surveillance and special surveys of all operational areas where such materials are used.
 Ensure compliance with applicable laws, license conditions, and Area-specific / customer radiation safety requirements, including support the Permit Holder in verifying and reporting safety compliance to BAPETEN.
 Maintain strict control over the use, storage, and disposal of radioactive materials to prevent unauthorized access or environmental contamination.
 Provide expert consultation on all aspects of radiation protection to personnel at all levels. This includes providing technical and administrative instructions to radiation workers to ensure proper implementation of safety procedures.
 Manage the receipt, inspection, and documentation of all incoming shipments of radioactive materials, as well as the preparation and shipment of outgoing materials in accordance with regulatory standards.
 Distribute and manage personnel monitoring equipment, assess the need for bioassay evaluations, maintain exposure and bioassay records, and notify individuals and supervisors of exposures approaching permissible limits with recommended remedial actions.
 Coordinate with Permit Holder to identify training needs, organize, and conduct radiation safety training programs and ensure personnel are properly instructed prior to using radioactive materials and whenever procedures, equipment, or regulations change.
 Exercise the authority to immediately suspend any activity or project that poses a threat to health, safety, or property. This includes executing emergency response actions during radiation-related incidents.
 Supervise and coordinate the radioactive waste disposal program, including the documentation of storage and disposal records and the monitoring of effluents.
 Maintain an accurate inventory of all radioactive materials and ensure that radionuclide quantities remain within licensed limits.
 Carry out sampling for leak tests of sealed radioactive sources and X-ray tubes and maintain corresponding records.
 Promote and implement the principles of ALARA (As Low as Reasonably Achievable) in all radiation-related activities to minimize exposure risks.
 Maintain comprehensive records related to radiation protection activities, including those not specifically designated above, such as receipt, transfer, survey, procedural documentation, evaluations, and compliance records.
 Regularly review and assess the effectiveness of the radiation protection and safety program and recommend improvements to enhance its implementation and outcomes.
 Ensure the availability, suitability, and proper use of radiation protection equipment, and monitor its condition and usage to maintain operational integrity.
 Implement access control measures in designated radiation-controlled areas to ensure that only authorized personnel are permitted entry.
 Prepare and submit periodic reports on the execution of Associate NDT Tech/ Radiation Associate Technician) duties, including documented evidence of compliance and performance, at least twice annually, as required by BAPETEN.


Qualifications

Essential Qualifications and Education:


 Holds BAPETEN Work Permit as RSO
 Snr. High School / Diploma.
 Minimum two plus (2+) years of technical or relevant experience.


About Us

Our ingenuity fuels daily life. Together, we’ve forged some of the most trusted partnerships across the energy value chain to make what was once just an idea a reality: laying subsea infrastructure thousands of feet below sea level, installing platforms hundreds of miles from shore, using our expertise to design and build offshore wind infrastructure, and reshaping the onshore landscape to deliver the energy products the world needs safely and sustainably.

For more than 100 years, we've been making the impossible possible. Today, we're driving the energy transition with more than 30,000 of the brightest minds across 54 countries.

Swiss Finance is hiring Investment Banking Analysts to work across M&A, capital markets, asset management, and hedge fund-related mandates, with direct exposure to institutional investors, deal analysis, and buy-side capital flows.




This role is designed for graduates and early-career professionals targeting careers in investment banking, private equity, hedge funds, or asset management, as well as career switchers from adjacent fields seeking a transition into high finance.




Role Overview

As an Investment Banking Analyst, you will support live M&A analysis, capital raising, and asset management workflows, while working closely with senior team members on investor-facing initiatives and buy-side research.




This role combines sell-side style execution with buy-side exposure, offering insight into how deals are evaluated, capital is raised, and investment decisions are made in practice.

You will serve as a bridge between technical execution and business strategy, ensuring that data is a core pillar of our mission to provide fair financial services to all Indonesians.




About Flip

Rafi, Luqman, and Anjar started Flip in 2015 to solve a simple problem: bank transfer fees. Today, Flip is a fintech pioneer helping millions of individuals and businesses move trillions of rupiah. Backed by Sequoia India, Insight Partners, and Insignia, our mission is to provide the most progressive and fairest financial services in the world.




We believe in fairness—not just for our customers, but for our employees. We provide a space where you can lead, innovate, and grow with equitable rewards and a culture of respect.




Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Leadership: Define the analytics roadmap and vision to support Flip’s long-term business goals in marketing and product development.
Team Management & Mentorship: Recruit, manage, and develop a high-performing team of data analysts. Foster a culture of ownership, continuous learning, and technical excellence.
Cross-Functional Partnership: Act as a strategic advisor to Marketing, Product, and Engineering leadership to identify growth opportunities and define "success" metrics.
Analytics Governance: Oversee the development of standardized frameworks for customer segmentation, lifetime value (LTV), and product performance.
Data Strategy: Partner with Data Engineering to influence data modeling, warehousing strategy, and the enrichment of our data ecosystem to ensure scalability.
Impact Measurement: Champion a rigorous experimentation culture, overseeing the design and analysis of complex A/B tests and causal impact studies.
Executive Communication: Synthesize complex analytical findings into compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for senior leadership and stakeholders.




Requirements:

Experience: 7+ years of experience in data analytics, with at least 2+ years in a formal people management or team lead role.
Technical Depth: Mastery of SQL and Python; expert-level experience with data visualization ecosystems (Looker, Tableau, or Power BI).
Business Acumen: Proven track record of using data to drive measurable business outcomes in user acquisition, retention, or monetization.
Methodology: Strong foundation in statistics, including A/B testing, cohort analysis, and predictive modeling.
Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with cloud-based data platforms (GCP/BigQuery preferred) and an understanding of data engineering workflows.
Communication: Exceptional stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence decision-making at the executive level.
Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a quantitative field (Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, etc.).




Nice to Have:

Experience in a Fintech environment or a high-growth startup.
Advanced knowledge of marketing attribution models and marketing mix modeling (MMM).
Experience overseeing data governance or data quality initiatives at scale.

title: Land and Sea Intern
job_description:
About the job

Location: Indonesia
City: Jakarta
Contract Type: Internship
Grade: Internship
Salary scale: Internship

End date of appointment

31 October 2026
Deadline: 25/04/2026
Job Reference: INT_ID055-E1-2

Please note that the deadline is based on Korean Standard Time Zone (KST, UTC+9)

INTRODUCTION TO GGGI

The Global Green Growth Institute (GGGI) is a treaty-based international, inter-governmental organization dedicated to supporting and promoting strong, inclusive and sustainable economic growth in developing countries and emerging economies. To learn more please visit about GGGI web page.

CURRENT OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for a talented and enthusiastic individual to work with us in the GGGI Indonesia Country Program’s Land and Sea Team. The intern will be responsible for providing operational and administrative assistance for the Party and Non-Party Stakeholders’ Capacity and Contribution to Accelerate the NDC Implementation (ID055 Project), funded by the NDC Partnership and working closely with the Ministry of Environment of the Republic of Indonesia to ensure prompt, responsive, accountable, and high-quality program delivery under these Plans.

OBJECTIVES

Under the supervision of the Sr. Officer, Blue Economy and working closely with Associate, Land and Sea, The Intern will assist in all of the administrative aspects in the event preparation and event implementation including budgeting, financial reporting, writing minutes of the meeting, translation and helping consultants with their administration. The intern is also encouraged to learn about NDC to provide assistance related to the content of the work.

PURPOSE

The Intern will assist in a wide range of administrative tasks and will be involved in the technical preparation of the ongoing work in the ID055 Project workstreams of GGGI. The intern will gain hands-on experience and training in many aspects of both administration, budgeting and management, while also gaining a good understanding of an inter-governmental organization as well as valuable exposure to NDC, green growth development, and climate change issue.


Support program activities with preparation for, or processing of, meetings or workshops including budgeting and financial reporting of the events. 
Support for the preparation of the events and meetings of the ID055 Project activities including confirming the participants, place, ToR and agenda preparation. 
Assist with events and meetings of the ID055 Project activities including note-taking, documentation, attendance list and settlement. 
Support Land and Sea Team and consultants on the procedures, policies and tools of travel, workshops and other administrative arrangements 
Carry out any other activities as may be directed by the supervisor. 
Provides general support to the project activities where necessary 
Any other support as needed in support of the Land and Sea Team 
Commit to working full-time (40 hours per week) from 1 May 2026 to 31 October 2026. 


ELIGIBILITY

Applicants to the GGGI internship program must at the time of application comply with all the following requirements:


Be in the final year of a Bachelor’s program, OR be enrolled in a Master's program, OR be a recent graduate (Bachelor or Master) within the last 12 months. 
Be engaged or recently engaged in academic study in a field directly related to GGGI’s work 
Possesses an excellent command of English and is able to produce written material. 


 

QUALIFICATION


A bachelor’s degree in international relations, public relations, communications, political science, or other related studies 
Having a minimum of 3 months of work experience would be desirable 
Strong oral and written English, and fluency in Indonesian is essential 
A pro-active attitude, with an ability to innovate in finding solutions 
Highly organized with an eye for detail and the ability to manage multiple tasks at the same time 
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work with multicultural and multidisciplinary teams 
Good level of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) 
Strong oral and written English, and fluency in Indonesian is essential 
Experience/knowledge of forestry and climate change issues (land-based mitigation) is an asset 


SELECTION PROCESS


Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered. 
GGGI evaluates applications based on: eligibility requirements, relevance of academic study and work experience (if any); and the level of interest and motivation to contribute to development work. 
Due to the high volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. 


IMPORTANT INFORMATION

Duration: The internship program is for six months. Once selected, interns must begin the internship either prior to or within 12 months of graduation.

Stipend: GGGI interns receive a monthly stipend of USD $500. All costs related to travel, insurance, accommodation, and living expenses must be borne by either the interns themselves or sponsoring institutions.

Travel: This position is based at GGGI Indonesia’s Jakarta Office. The intern will arrange and finance their travel to the internship location.

Medical Insurance: Interns must present proof of valid medical insurance to GGGI upon returning the signed internship contract.

Confidentiality: Interns must keep confidential any and all unpublished information obtained during the course of the internship and may not publish any documents based on such information.

Academic Credit: Interns may get academic credit from their institution of higher education for the internship. Interns need to check with their university to confirm their academic credit policy for internships.

Working Hours: Intern are expected to work full-time (40 hours per week) during normal working hours. Leave should be pre-approved by the Intern Manager, and the latter should inform the Office of HR accordingly.

Learning Outcomes: before completion of the internship, Intern Managers are expected to review the learning outcomes with the intern.

Certificate: provided upon successful completion of the internship, upon request.

Application Deadline: 25 April 2026

title: Associate, Indonesia
job_description:
About the job

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.

What You'll Do

As a member of our consulting team, you’ll work alongside some of the world’s top minds on cases that reshape business, government, and society. You’ll collaborate on challenging projects with team members from many backgrounds and disciplines, increasing your understanding of complex business problems from diverse perspectives and developing new skills and experience to help you at every stage of your career—at BCG and beyond.

Consulting work is varied and rigorous, much of it performed at our client sites. Projects can vary in length, size, and location, depending on the client’s challenge. Because our clients operate all over the world, you may travel internationally.

If you are joining us directly from school or with a few years of experience, expect to spend time working across a wide range of clients and projects from helping to devise the integration strategy for a pharmaceutical merger to developing sustainable farming practices in emerging countries.

For more experienced professionals, or as your career advances, you’ll begin to specialize in one or more practice areas, perhaps as part of our expert consulting track for those with deep knowledge and skills in a particular discipline or industry. Every step of the way you will be guided by BCG mentors and world-class learning programs designed to meet your individual needs.

BCG offers a comprehensive benefits program, including medical, dental and vision coverage, telemedicine services, life, accident and disability insurance, parental leave and family planning benefits, caregiving resources, mental health offerings, a generous retirement program, financial guidance, paid time off, and more.

What You'll Bring


Work experience of 0-3 years in top tier firms
Outstanding academic performance and strong record of extracurricular activity
Strong research/analysis experience and abilities, including excellent Excel skills
Able to work creatively and analytically
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.

title: Asset Operations Specialist

Founded in 2018, MEXC is a global cryptocurrency exchange serving millions of users worldwide. We are committed to building a secure, efficient, and accessible platform for digital asset trading.

Built on high-performance infrastructure and a user-first philosophy, MEXC delivers fast execution, strong market depth, and a seamless trading experience for global users.




At MEXC, we believe the best platforms are built through constant improvement and open collaboration. We value direct communication, practical thinking, and individuals who take ownership of their ideas. As the digital asset industry continues to evolve, we remain focused on innovation, transparency, and global expansion while building a trusted platform for the next generation of financial technology.

title: Data Collector
job_description:
About the job

Get paid to submit photos and screenshots of expired loyalty cards, event tickets, gift cards, and coupons with barcodes. Simple task, remote, global participation.




We are hiring freelance contributors worldwide to help us collect real photos and screenshots of expired cards, passes, and tickets with barcodes for a global research project.

This is a remote, flexible, task-based project where you submit images you already own or can easily capture.




📌 What you will submit

You will upload photos or screenshots of:

Membership / Loyalty cards (gym, library, store rewards, etc.)
Event tickets / passes (concerts, sports, exhibitions, conferences, etc.)
Gift cards (expired or used only)
Coupons (expired or already used)

**Every item must contain a visible barcode.

***Only real data — no AI-generated or internet images allowed.




📌 Basic Requirements

Must be 18+ years old
Must own the cards/tickets you submit
Cards/passes must be expired, used, or zero balance
Barcode must be clearly visible and readable
Images must be original and not downloaded from the internet




📌 Image Guidelines (Simple)

Take clear photos in good lighting
Show the full card/pass — no cropping
Different backgrounds encouraged (table, hand, wall, indoor/outdoor, etc.)
Screenshots must show merchant name + barcode
Do NOT use Apple Wallet screenshots
Accepted formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG, HEIC

📌 Do's:

Barcode should be visible and not obstructed by any element
Merchant name should be visible along with barcode, else capture other side where Merchant name is given
All Physical assets needs to be in jpg, jpeg, png and heic extensions only
Location on Phone should be switched on to capture EXIF Data (Latitude and Longitude)
Do not edit Photos post capturing which will remove details of exif
Physical assets are only Photo Images and not Screenshots or picture of picture
The orientation of the physical image should be straight and not curved or bended

📌 Dont's:

Photos captured should be strictly from Physical cards
No paper cards or printed cards are allowed
Strictly avoid Web scraping or fabrication of assets
Assets created using generated AI or derived from synthetic data will be rejected
Exif data is mandatory and any Photo with no exif data will be rejected




🌍 Target Countries

We are especially looking for contributors from:

China, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Japan, South Korea, Vietnam, Turkey, Israel, Thailand, Malaysia, India, Brazil, Portugal, France, Germany, Spain, Italy, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Netherlands, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and more.




💰 Payment

Paid per accepted asset
More valid submissions = higher total payout




📩 How To Apply

Step 1 - Apply by registering on our Gig-Sourcing Platform (Click Here)

Step 2 – Apply for the project “Vector Bar Codes Global – Mar 2026”

Step 3 - Communication & Support Channel - https://discord.gg/WrtbhUZMU

Step 4 – Download data collection mobile app – AIDAC (Available for iOS & Android)

Step 5 – Video for AIDAC usage - https://app.box.com/s/3d8xlbhw32qt5x6sy50wcbk39bgyw7ur

Step 6 – Use the same email ID for data submission on AIDAC

Step 7 – Get started 😊

**Spots are limited per country, so early registration is recommended.

title: R Engineer
job_description:
About the job

As a remote, hourly paid R Engineer, you will review AI-generated responses and generate high-quality R and data-analysis-focused content, evaluating the reasoning quality and step-by-step problem-solving behind each solution. You will assess solutions for accuracy, clarity, and adherence to the prompt; identify errors in statistical methodology or data-wrangling workflows; fact-check analytical results; write expert-level explanations and model solutions that demonstrate correct use of R; and rate and compare multiple AI responses based on correctness and reasoning quality. This contract role is with SME Careers, a fast-growing AI Data Services company and subsidiary of SuperAnnotate that provides AI training data for many of the world’s largest AI companies and foundation model labs, directly helping to improve the world’s premier AI models.

Key Responsibilities


 Develop AI Training Content: Create detailed prompts in various topics and responses to guide AI learning, ensuring the models reflect a comprehensive understanding of diverse subjects.
 Optimize AI Performance: Evaluate and rank AI responses to enhance the model's accuracy, fluency, and contextual relevance.
 Ensure Model Integrity: Test AI models for potential inaccuracies or biases, validating their reliability across use cases.


Your Profile


 2+ years of hands-on experience using R for data analysis, statistics, or data science work.
 Strong proficiency in R programming, including data wrangling, functional programming patterns, and writing reusable functions or packages.
 Solid grounding in applied statistics, including regression, inference, and model validation, with practical experience implementing these methods in R.
 Experience building end-to-end analyses in R that include data cleaning, exploratory analysis, modeling, and visualization.
 Familiarity with common R ecosystems such as tidyverse, data.table, and ggplot2, and the ability to choose appropriate tools for a given task.
 Professional experience in a data-focused role such as data scientist, statistician, quantitative analyst, or similar.
 Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a closely related quantitative field.
 Significant experience using large language models (LLMs) to assist with coding, analysis design, and code review in R.
 Excellent English writing skills with the ability to document analyses and explain complex statistical ideas clearly to non-experts.
 Previous experience with AI data training or model evaluation is strongly preferred, and Minimum C1 English proficiency is required.



Desired Skills and Experience

R programming, Statistical modeling, Data analysis, Data visualization, Reproducible research, Data cleaning, Time series analysis, Machine learning in R, LLM coding, English
